pi_items.source: Task description states: 'Add a new encounter type with the following details: Name: Telehealth Consultation'

Task description specifies description as: 'Remote patient consultation conducted via video or phone call for telehealth program'. All four keywords (remote, consultation, video, phone) appear in this description. Verifier.py checks for 'remote' and 'consultation' as required keywords at lines 84-85.

Task description states: 'Navigate to the OpenMRS Administration page (accessible via the OpenMRS link or by appending /openmrs/admin to the URL)'. Confirmed by multiple other task.json files in the same environment which all specify this same admin_url. Task setup scripts use BAHMNI_BASE_URL and OPENMRS_BASE_URL defined in task_utils.sh.

OpenMRS REST API documentation (rest.openmrs.org) specifies the encounter type endpoint as /ws/rest/v1/encountertype. In the context of this system, OPENMRS_API_URL is defined as /openmrs/ws/rest/v1 in task_utils.sh (line 10). Therefore, relative to OPENMRS_API_URL, /encountertype is the correct endpoint. Confirmed by setup_task.sh (line 21) and export_result.sh (line 18) which call openmrs_api_get '/encountertype?q=Telehealth+Consultation&v=default'

privileged_info_summary: The task creates an encounter type named 'Telehealth Consultation' with a description containing the keywords 'remote', 'consultation', 'video', and 'phone'. The admin interface is accessed at https://localhost/openmrs/admin (the OpenMRS administration page). The REST API endpoint for encounter types is /encountertype, which resolves to /openmrs/ws/rest/v1/encountertype when combined with the base OpenMRS API URL. All metadata values are verified against task documentation, OpenMRS REST API specifications, and the task's shell scripts.
